# Descriptive Statistics of Crashes
This section provides an overview of the crashes included in the analysis dataset. It highlights the distribution of crashes by mode,
severity, functional classification, and year.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: true

import pandas as pd
from report_utils.table_utils import (
    format_count,
    format_currency,
    format_percent,
    format_miles,
    render_markdown_table,
    split_count_percent_tables,
)
from report_utils.graph_utils import render_plotly_figure
from report_utils.render_guard import render_guard
from src.utils import dashboard_func
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

modes_and_display_names = {
    "All Crashes": "All Crashes",
    "ped": "Pedestrian",
    "bike": "Bicycle",
    "mv": "Motor Vehicle",
}

# load dashboard data
dashboard_data = None
y0 = y1 = None
_DASHBOARD_DATA_LOADED = False
with render_guard("Dashboard data could not be loaded for this study."):
    dashboard_data = dashboard_func.get_dashboard_data(study_id)
    y0, y1 = dashboard_func.get_year_range(dashboard_data)
    _DASHBOARD_DATA_LOADED = True
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: true

if _DASHBOARD_DATA_LOADED:
    display(Markdown("## Crashes by Mode and Severity"))
    with render_guard("Mode by severity breakdown could not be rendered."):
        table, fig = dashboard_func.mode_by_severity_stacked_components(
            dashboard_data, y0=y0, y1=y1
        )

        if not table.data:
            display(Markdown("_No crash data available for mode by severity breakdown._"))
        else:
            cols = [c["id"] for c in table.columns]
            table_df = pd.DataFrame.from_records(table.data, columns=cols)

            render_plotly_figure(fig)
            render_markdown_table(table_df)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: true

if _DASHBOARD_DATA_LOADED:
    display(Markdown("## Crashes by Severity"))
    for mode, display_name in modes_and_display_names.items():
        display(Markdown(f"### Crashes by Severity - {display_name}\n"))

        with render_guard(f"No data available for {display_name} Crashes."):
            table, fig = dashboard_func.create_donut_fig_table(
                df=dashboard_data,
                group_col="crash_severity",
                y0=None,
                y1=None,
                filter=mode,
            )

            if not table.data:
                display(Markdown(f"_No data available for {display_name}._"))
                continue

            cols = [c["id"] for c in table.columns]
            table_df = pd.DataFrame.from_records(table.data, columns=cols)

            render_plotly_figure(fig, height=450)
            render_markdown_table(table_df, smaller=True)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: true

if _DASHBOARD_DATA_LOADED:
    display(Markdown("## Crashes by Functional Classification"))
    for mode, display_name in modes_and_display_names.items():
        display(Markdown(f"### Crashes by Functional Classification - {display_name}\n"))

        with render_guard(f"No data available for {display_name} Crashes."):
            table, fig = dashboard_func.funclass_stacked_components(
                df=dashboard_data,
                y0=None,
                y1=None,
                filtered_mode=mode,
            )

            if not table.data:
                display(Markdown(f"_No data available for {display_name}._"))
                continue

            cols = [c["id"] for c in table.columns]
            table_df = pd.DataFrame.from_records(table.data, columns=cols)
            count_df, percent_df = split_count_percent_tables(table_df)

            render_plotly_figure(fig)
            render_markdown_table(count_df, smaller=True)
            render_markdown_table(percent_df, smaller=True)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: true
def _coerce_col_id(col_id: str):
    try:
        return int(col_id)
    except ValueError:
        return col_id

if _DASHBOARD_DATA_LOADED:
    display(Markdown("## Crashes by Year"))
    for mode, display_name in modes_and_display_names.items():
        display(Markdown(f"### Crashes by Year - {display_name}\n"))

        with render_guard(f"No data available for {display_name} Crashes."):
            table, fig = dashboard_func.severity_year_grouped_components(
                df=dashboard_data,
                y0=None,
                y1=None,
                filtered_mode=mode,
            )

            if not table.data:
                display(Markdown(f"_No data available for {display_name}._"))
                continue

            cols = [_coerce_col_id(c["id"]) for c in table.columns]
            table_df = pd.DataFrame.from_records(table.data, columns=cols)
            table_df.rename(columns=lambda c: str(c), inplace=True)  # optional, keeps them strings afterwards

            render_plotly_figure(fig, height=600)
            render_markdown_table(table_df)
```
